question_text: A bat uses echolocation in a cave. It emits a sound at 50 kHz and the echo returns after 0.12 seconds. The speed of sound in air is 340 m/s. Which statement correctly explains why the bat hears an echo, and approximately how far away is the object?

options.label: A
options.text: The sound reflects off a solid surface and returns; the object is about 20.4 m away

options.label: B
options.text: The sound refracts through multiple air layers and bounces back; the object is about 40.8 m away

options.label: C
options.text: The sound is absorbed by the cave walls and re-radiated; the object is about 10.2 m away

options.label: D
options.text: The sound scatters from dust particles in the air; the object is about 5.1 m away

explanation: Echoes occur when sound reflects (bounces) off solid surfaces. Total distance = 340 × 0.12 = 40.8 m for the round trip. Distance to object = 40.8 ÷ 2 = 20.4 m. The frequency (50 kHz) is a distractor—echoes work at any frequency!
